data.title: NOAA finds TMC USA application in full compliance, 65,000 km2 area, 619 Mt nodules
data.description:
TMC announced NOAA determined its consolidated TMC USA application is in full compliance, advancing the project toward a potential permit by Q1 2027.

Key Highlights:

  • NOAA found TMC USA’s consolidated exploration and commercial recovery application fully compliant with DSHMRA rules.
  • Consolidated filing expands commercial recovery area from ~25,000 to ~65,000 km2 with estimated 619 Mt wet nodules.
  • Application moves to certification and Federal Register posting, then draft EIS and public comment stages.
  • TMC expects the regulatory process could conclude before the end of Q1 2027, enabling potential permit issuance.

Original SEC Filing: TMC the metals Co Inc. [ TMC ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Greenidge Generation Holdings Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 29, 2026, Nasdaq notified the company that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5605(c)(2)(A) because its audit committee has fewer than three independent directors following an April 15, 2026 resignation. The company will use the cure period under Rule 5605(c)(4)(B) until the earlier of its next annual meeting or April 15, 2027 (or October 12, 2026 if the meeting occurs earlier) and is recruiting an independent director; the notice has no immediate effect on trading.

Original SEC Filing: Greenidge Generation Holdings Inc. [ GREE ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Gates Industrial reports Q1 2026: revenue $851.1M, adjusted EBITDA $177.4M, reiterates 2026 guidance
data.description:
Gates Industrial reported first-quarter 2026 results with net sales of $851.1 million and adjusted EBITDA of $177.4 million, while reiterating full-year 2026 guidance. Net income attributable to shareholders was $59.7 million, or $0.23 per diluted share, and adjusted net income per diluted share was $0.35. The company highlighted solid order rates, a book-to-bill above 1 and successful implementation of a new ERP system in Europe.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales of $851.1 million for the three months ended March 28, 2026 (up 0.4% year-over-year).
  • Gross profit: $338.0 million; gross profit margin 39.7% for the quarter.
  • Operating income from continuing operations: $109.9 million.
  • Net income attributable to shareholders: $59.7 million; diluted earnings per share $0.23.
  • Adjusted measures: Adjusted EBITDA of $177.4 million (20.8% margin); Adjusted Net Income per diluted share $0.35.

Business Highlights

  • Operational execution: Completed implementation of a new enterprise resource planning (ERP) system in Europe during the quarter.
  • Order momentum: Exited the quarter with solid order rates and a book-to-bill ratio above 1, indicating demand strength versus shipments.
  • Segment performance: Power Transmission reported net sales of $533.2 million (1.1% increase year-over-year) with adjusted EBITDA of $112.0 million; Fluid Power reported net sales of $317.9 million (down 0.8% year-over-year) with adjusted EBITDA of $65.4 million.
  • Cash and balance sheet: Cash and cash equivalents of $785.3 million and management cited a well-positioned balance sheet to support strategic objectives.
  • Capital deployment and guidance: Reiterated 2026 full-year targets including core sales growth of 1%–4%, adjusted EBITDA $775M–$835M, adjusted EPS $1.52–$1.68, and capital expenditures of approximately $120 million.

Original SEC Filing: Gates Industrial Corp plc [ GTES ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Estée Lauder reports Q3 revenue $3.71B, adjusted EPS $0.91 and raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
Estée Lauder reported fiscal 2026 third-quarter net sales of $3.712 billion and organic net sales of $3.611 billion, reflecting 5% reported growth and 2% organic growth year-over-year. Adjusted diluted net earnings per share rose to $0.91 (GAAP diluted EPS $0.24) and adjusted operating margin expanded to 15.0%. The company raised its fiscal 2026 outlook and provided a preliminary view for fiscal 2027 with targeted net sales growth of 3%–5% and an adjusted operating margin near 13%.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue (Net Sales): $3,712 million for the three months ended March 31, 2026 (5% reported increase vs. prior year).
  • Organic Net Sales (Non-GAAP): $3,611 million (2% organic growth vs. prior year).
  • Gross Profit: $2,836 million; Gross Margin: 76.4% (up from 75.0% year-over-year).
  • Operating Income: $249 million (operating margin 6.7%); Adjusted Operating Income (Non-GAAP): $557 million with Adjusted Operating Margin 15.0%.
  • Net Earnings and EPS: GAAP diluted net earnings per common share $0.24; Adjusted diluted net earnings per common share (Non-GAAP) $0.91.

Business Highlights

  • Category and brand momentum: Double-digit year-to-date growth in Fragrance; notable brand performance from La Mer, TOM FORD, Le Labo and The Ordinary contributed to share gains in key markets.
  • Regional performance: Net sales growth in three of four regions led by Mainland China (high single-digit organic growth) and double-digit growth across Priority Emerging Markets in EUKEM and The Americas.
  • Beauty Reimagined & PRGP execution: Profit Recovery and Growth Plan delivered adjusted gross margin expansion and operating leverage; restructuring approvals increased expected program scope and benefits.
  • Restructuring program update: Cumulative restructuring charges of $1.1 billion recognized through March 31, 2026; projected total restructuring and other charges now $1.5–1.7 billion with expected annual gross benefits of $1.0–1.2 billion and a final net reduction in positions of 9,000–10,000.
  • Major commercial and distribution initiatives: Expanded presence on Amazon and TikTok Shop across multiple markets, launches in specialty-multi channels (Sephora), freestanding store openings for Le Labo and TOM FORD, strategic partnerships with Shopify, Accenture and WPP, and a minority investment in 111Skin.

Original SEC Filing: ESTEE LAUDER COMPANIES INC [ EL ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Esperion Therapeutics to Be Acquired by Essence Parent for $3.16 Per Share Plus CVR Up to $100M
data.description:
Esperion Therapeutics entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Essence Parent under which shareholders will receive $3.16 in cash per share plus one contingent value right (CVR) tied to sales milestones worth up to $100 million in aggregate. Essence MergerCo will merge into Esperion, which will become a wholly owned subsidiary of Essence Parent. The deal is subject to stockholder and regulatory approvals and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026. The CVR pays based on 2027 U.S. bempedoic acid product sales and future U.S. bumetanide product sales through 2030.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Merger
  • Counterparty: Essence Parent
  • Signed / Effective: May 01 2026 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing; Outside Date Nov 01 2026
  • Reason: Provide cash exit with additional CVR upside

Original SEC Filing: Esperion Therapeutics, Inc. [ ESPR ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: Aldeyra Therapeutics updates reproxalap NDA data, outlines FDA meeting and AbbVie option
data.description:
Aldeyra Therapeutics released an updated corporate overview disclosing standardized efficacy estimates for reproxalap trials and its interpretations of FDA positions.

Key Highlights:

  • Furnished Corporate Overview (Exhibit 99.1) showing standardized treatment estimates and CIs for primary endpoints of reproxalap trials submitted in the dry eye disease NDA.
  • Summarized FDA positions and Aldeyra’s abbreviated interpretations on primary endpoints; Aldeyra asserts majority of trials support reproxalap efficacy.
  • Noted FDA issued a Complete Response Letter citing insufficient substantial evidence of efficacy; Type A meeting with FDA expected Q2 2026 to discuss the letter.
  • Outlined exclusive AbbVie option: $100M upfront less option fees, $100M approval milestone, $200M additional milestones, 60/40 profit split in U.S., and tiered royalties outside U.S.
  • Provided pipeline and milestones: ADX-2191 Phase 3 and Phase 2/3 initiations expected H1 2026; ADX-248 and ADX-246 IND/planned trials expected in 2026; cash runway noted to fund into 2028 (company guidance).

Original SEC Filing: Aldeyra Therapeutics, Inc. [ ALDX ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

data.title: UK defence firm Ultra agrees deal with prosecutors over failure to prevent bribery
data.description:
The UK's Serious Fraud Office has reached a deferred prosecution agreement with British defence contractor Ultra Electronics for failure to prevent bribery, it said on Friday.

Ultra, which had referred itself to the SFO, agreed to pay 10 million pounds ($13.6 million) after accepting responsibility, the agency said.

The company – which in 2022 was acquired by rival Cobham, owned by U.S. private equity firm Advent – did not immediately respond to an emailed request for comment.

The SFO said the deal, which ends its criminal investigation, relates to three public sector contracts in Algeria and Oman that Ultra sought through the use of agents.

One was a contract worth up to 200 million pounds awarded by the Omani Ministry of Transport and Communications, while the other two related to Algeria and were not ultimately secured by Ultra, the SFO said.

Under the deal, Ultra will also pay the SFO's investigation costs of 4.8 million pounds and provide annual reports for the next three years on its anti-bribery and compliance programme.

($1 = 0.7342 pounds)

data.title: Spirit Airlines prepares to cease operations after rescue deal stalls, WSJ reports
data.description:
Bankrupt discount carrier Spirit Airlines is preparing to cease operations, after failing to align certain bondholders and the U.S. government on a bailout plan, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Spirit Airlines and the White House did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

A rescue hearing scheduled for Thursday, April 30, did not take place after talks over the terms of a potential $500 million U.S. government bailout reached an impasse.

data.title: Nigeria's Dangote refinery issues spot tender for crude
data.description:
The Nigerian Dangote refinery issued a short five-day spot tender for crude this week to buy for the month of June, according to a document seen by Reuters.

Africa's largest refinery, it became fully operational at the start of this year and company has previously said it could source only about five to seven crude cargoes a month locally, far short of the 13–15 it requires.

The tender, which was issued on Wednesday, was calling for any West African, Mediterranean, Guyanese, Brazilian and U.S. grades by May 5, 10 A.M. BST.

The refinery did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Friday is a public holiday in Nigeria.

data.title: Top AI Companies Agree to Pentagon Deals for Classified Work — WSJ
data.description:
By Amrith Ramkumar

The Defense Department has completed agreements with seven technology companies, including many of the industry's biggest, to use their artificial-intelligence capabilities in classified settings, boosting the Pentagon's efforts to gain access to cutting-edge AI tools.

The department said Friday it is now capable of using in classified settings the technology and models from the ChatGPT maker, OpenAI; Alphabet's Google; Elon Musk's SpaceX; Microsoft; Amazon; Nvidia; and a startup, Reflection AI. SpaceX owns Musk's AI company, xAI.

While some of the companies, including OpenAI and SpaceX, had initial deals with the Pentagon agreeing to have their AI tools used by the military in all lawful scenarios, completing the contracts is an important step toward embedding them in day-to-day operations. The deals show how much of Silicon Valley is agreeing to the Defense Department's terms in a way that Anthropic didn't when it rejected the Pentagon's contract earlier this year in what spiraled into a monthslong feud. The deals also highlight how the Pentagon is racing to incorporate AI into its systems.

For many months, Anthropic's Claude models were some of the only tools available in classified settings through the data-mining company Palantir Technologies, which offers AI tools to the military through its platform Maven. In response to a contract dispute, the Pentagon declared Anthropic a supply-chain risk unsuitable for military work, increasing the urgency of offering other models to servicemembers in classified settings. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, while testifying in Congress on Thursday, called Anthropic Chief Executive Dario Amodei an "ideological lunatic."

Emil Michael, undersecretary of defense for research and engineering and a former tech executive, said in a statement: "We are equipping the warfighter with a suite of AI tools to maintain an unfair advantage and achieve absolute decision superiority."

Microsoft and Amazon, two of the largest providers of cloud-computing infrastructure and leading partners to model developers, already have deep relationships with the Pentagon as well as their own AI tools.

The Pentagon's deals with Nvidia and Reflection are new and highlight a desire to offer open-source models, the details of which are publicly available to developers. Most leading AI models are closed, limiting how users can customize them.

Nvidia, the world's biggest chip company, develops its own open models. Its agreement with the Pentagon covers its Nemotron open-source models, which support AI agents capable of carrying out tasks on their own.

Nvidia Chief Executive Jensen Huang, who has cultivated ties with President Trump, has said he thinks open models are better than closed models in many national-security contexts because their attributes are fully known and they can easily be tailored for specialized use cases. "Safety and security is frankly enhanced with open-source," he said in a recent conversation with the head of the Special Competitive Studies Project think tank that was posted online.

Leading AI companies in China specialize in open models that they hope to sell to other countries, adding to the importance of developing U.S. alternatives, administration officials said.

Nvidia is an investor in Reflection, which specializes in open-source models and has forged close ties with the Trump administration. Led by former researchers at Google's DeepMind lab, the company is involved in a deal supported by the government to develop models tailored to people in South Korea and is in talks to raise money from investors at a $25 billion valuation. The company hasn't yet released any AI models.

"This shared understanding with the Pentagon is a first step in supporting U.S. national security, and sets a precedent for how AI labs could work across the U.S. government — from supporting our servicemembers to our scientists," a Reflection spokeswoman said.

Anthropic is fighting the administration's ban on use of its software for defense work in two separate legal cases. The company's models have been used during the Iran war and the operation to capture then-Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro earlier this year.

Many of the companies with newly completed agreements have said their Defense Department deals include commitments that their tools wouldn't be used for mass surveillance or autonomous weapons. The Pentagon has said that it wouldn't carry out those illegal activities and that companies should trust the military to use AI responsibly.

Write to Amrith Ramkumar at amrith.ramkumar@wsj.com

data.title: Terex Corp 1Q 2026: Revenue $1.73B, EPS $(0.93) — 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Terex Corp reported first-quarter 2026 results marked by strong revenue growth driven by the February 2026 REV acquisition and higher volumes across material processing and aerial work platforms, while the company posted a net loss for the quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $1.73B for Q1 2026, up from $1.23B in Q1 2025; YoY change 41.1%.
  • Net income was $(89)M (net loss) for Q1 2026, versus $21M net income in Q1 2025.
  • Diluted EPS was $(0.93) for Q1 2026, versus $0.31 in Q1 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven largely by the Feb. 2, 2026 REV acquisition, which contributed about $436M in sales and strengthened the Specialty Vehicles offering.
  • North America now represents roughly 80% of sales after a strategic shift toward U.S.-based, resilient end markets and integration of REV.
  • Material Processing (MP) and Aerial Work Platforms/Equipment Services (ES) delivered volume-driven gains.
  • Backlog increased to $7.1B; Terex deployed its Terex Operating System to pursue cost reductions.
  • Working capital improved to 16.7% of trailing annualized pro forma sales through receivables management and factoring efforts.

Original SEC Filing: TEREX CORP [ TEX ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

data.title: SCI Engineered Materials, Inc. 1Q 2026: Revenue $8.16M, EPS $0.1— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
SCI Engineered Materials, Inc. reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue rising to $8.16M and net income of $462262 compared with $3.5M in revenue and $309717 in net income a year earlier; diluted EPS was $0.1 versus $0.07 in Q1 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $8.16M for Q1 2026, up from $3.50M in Q1 2025; YoY change 133.2%.
  • Net income was $462,262 for Q1 2026, compared with $309,717 in Q1 2025; YoY change 49.2%.
  • Diluted EPS was $0.10 for Q1 2026, versus $0.07 in Q1 2025; YoY change 42.9%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by higher volumes, product mix improvements and pass-through of raw material price increases.
  • New products and market traction included a launch of conductive ITO and rotatable long targets, and increased adoption of enriched boron carbide in defense and aerospace applications.
  • The company identified a niche additive-manufacturing opportunity for spherical powders and is pursuing custom powder solutions.
  • Operational capacity is being expanded with ramp-ups of vacuum hot presses, a cold isostatic press, kilns and specialty diffusion bonding to support higher production.
  • Higher inventories and customer deposits late in the quarter reflect increased orders and stronger near-term demand.

data.title: IES Holdings 3Q 2026: Revenue $974.3M, EPS $5.44— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
IES Holdings, Inc. reported third-quarter 2026 results with consolidated revenue of $974.3M and diluted EPS of $5.44, reflecting year‑over‑year revenue growth and higher profitability versus the prior-year quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $974.3M for Q3 2026 (three months ended Mar 31, 2026), up from $834.0M in the year-ago quarter; YoY change 16.8%.

Business Highlights

  • Strong data center demand supported backlog and execution in Communications, Infrastructure Solutions and Commercial & Industrial businesses.
  • Residential revenues declined about 9–10% due to single‑family weakness, pricing pressure and lower multi‑family backlog.
  • Acquisition of Gulf Island expanded manufacturing capacity and added meaningful backlog and remaining performance obligations for Infrastructure Solutions.
  • Gross margins improved year to date to 25.7% driven by better pricing, project execution and productivity, offsetting residential pressures.

data.title: Roku Blows Past Estimates as Platform Growth Accelerates
data.description:
Roku, Inc. rose 7.42% intraday after reporting Q1 2026 results that beat Wall Street's estimates. Total net revenue came in at $1.25 billion, up 22% year over year and above the $1.204 billion consensus, while diluted EPS of $0.57 nearly doubled the $0.33 expected. Net income was $86 million, against a $27 million loss a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA hit $148 million, up 165%, and free cash flow on a trailing twelve-month basis reached an all-time high of $539 million, up 81%.

The platform growth was broad. Advertising brought in $613 million, up 27%, with Roku's video ad growth outpacing both the U.S. OTT and broader digital ad markets. Programmatic spend through third-party partners jumped more than 40% year over year. The number of advertisers on Roku Ads Manager more than doubled. Subscriptions added $519 million, up 30%, the highest quarter ever for Premium Subscription sign-ups. Roku also crossed 100 million streaming households worldwide during April, the first time its TV operating system has reached that scale.

Devices told a different story. Revenue fell 16% to $118 million, with gross margin of negative 16.3%, driven by lower player unit sales and promotional pricing. Roku bought back $100 million of shares in Q1. For Q2, Roku guided total net revenue of $1.3 billion and adjusted EBITDA of $170 million. Full-year revenue guidance was raised to $5.5 billion, with adjusted EBITDA of $675 million, roughly 330 basis points of margin expansion year over year.

data.title: S&P 500 Breakout -- Why History Says the Rally Isn't Over
data.description:
The S&P 500 rose 10% in April and is up more than 5% this year, a setup that may favor additional gains, according to a Friday note from Carson Group.

Ryan Detrick, chief market strategist at the firm, said historical patterns lean positive when the index enters May with a gain above 5%. He said the benchmark finished the year higher in 23 of the past 25 similar cases, suggesting momentum often persists after a strong spring.

Detrick also pointed out that the S&P 500 has already set 11 record highs in 2026. He said no year in market history has ended with exactly 11 new highs, which he views as another sign the rally could continue.

Still, the strategist framed the call as a probability, not a certainty. He said the current bull market may have more room to run if broad market momentum holds through the rest of 2026. The note did not offer a year-end target, but it argued the setup has favored buyers in past cycles.

data.title: Imperial Oil Files 8K - Regulation FD >IMO
data.description:
Imperial Oil Ltd. (IMO) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on May 01, 2026.

On May 1, 2026, Imperial Oil Limited (the "company") by means of a press release announced that the board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of 87 cents per share on the outstanding common shares of the company for the second quarter of 2026. The dividend is payable on July 1, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on June 4, 2026. A copy of the press release is attached as Exhibit 99.1 to this report.
